options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Choose the completion goal.
Ripto - Collect enough orbs to open the arena and beat Ripto.
64 Orb - Collect 64 orbs and beat Ripto.
100 Percent - Collect all talismans, orbs, and gems and beat Ripto.
    In Open World mode, no talismans are required.
10 Tokens - Collect 8000 gems and 55 orbs to unlock the theme park
    and collect all 10 tokens in Dragon Shores.
All Skillpoints - Collect all 16 skill points in the game.
Epilogue - Unlock the full epilogue by collecting all 16 skill points
    and defeating Ripto.

options.name: guaranteed_items
options.description: Guarantees that the specified items will be in the item pool
options: Moneybags Unlock - Wall by Aquaria Towers

options.name: death_link
options.description:
Spyro will die when a DeathLink is
received and will send them on his death.
Not every death will trigger a DeathLink,
and not every received DeathLink will kill Spyro.

options.name: enable_25_pct_gem_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting 25% of the gems in a level

options.name: enable_50_pct_gem_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting 50% of the gems in a level

options.name: enable_75_pct_gem_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting 75% of the gems in a level

options.name: enable_gem_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting all gems in a level

options.name: enable_total_gem_checks
options.description:
Adds checks for every 500 gems you collect total.
Gems currently paid to Moneybags do not count
towards your total. Logic assumes you pay Moneybags everywhere
you can so you cannot be locked out of checks.

options.name: max_total_gem_checks
options.display_name: Max for Total Gem Count Checks
options.description:
The highest number of total gems that can be required
for Total Gem Count checks. Has no effect if
Enable Total Gem Count Checks is disabled.

options.name: enable_skillpoint_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting skill points

options.name: enable_life_bottle_checks
options.description: Adds checks for getting life bottles

options.name: enable_spirit_particle_checks
options.description:
Adds checks for getting all spirit particles in a level.
Some minigame enemies are counted as spirit particles,
like Draclets in Crystal Glacier. Some enemies only release
spirit particles while the camera is on them.

options.name: enable_gemsanity
options.description:
Adds checks for each individual gem.
If Moneybagssanity is off, all Moneybags prices will be set to 0 in game.
Full requires the host to edit allow_full_gemsanity
    in their yaml file, as it is very disruptive.
Off: Individual gems are not checks.
Partial: Only some number of randomly chosen gems become checks. For every
    level with loose gems (not speedways), Gem Bundle items giving
    gems for that level will be added to the pool.
Full: All gems are checks.

options.name: gemsanity_item_locations
options.description:
Should gem items be shuffled only
in your world, or spread across the entire multiworld.
Global is more disruptive to other players.

options.name: gemsanity_reward_type
options.description:
Adds item rewards to the pool to match the applicable gem locations.
Gems: If Gemsanity is set to FULL, each gem location in the game will have
    its individual matching gem added to the item pool. Applies only to
    full gemsanity. Choosing this slows down generation significantly.
Bundles: Adds items that grant a number of gems for an individual level
    to the item pool.
    If Gemsanity=FULL, extra gem locations may contain other/filler items.
    If Gemsanity=PARTIAL, This option is selected automatically

options.name: gemsanity_gem_bundle_size
options.description:
Define the amount of gems awarded by each Gem Bundle item.
Determines how many items are added to the item pool.
Determines how many gem locations are randomly selected if
    Gemsanity is set to Partial (adds one location per bundle item).
WARNING: Selecting an option smaller than 25 requires the host to
    edit allow_full_gemsanity in their yaml file.
Default (50):adds 8 Gem Bundles per Level to the Item Pool (168 Total)
10: 40 Bundles/Level (840 Total) ,  16: 25 Bundles/level (525 Total)
20: 20 Bundles/Level (420 Total) ,  25: 16 Bundles/Level (336 Total)
40: 10 Bundles/Level (210 Total) ,  50: 8 Bundles/Level (168 Total)
80: 5 Bundles/Level (105 Total)  ,  100: 4 Bundles/Level (84 Total)

options.name: ripto_door_orbs
options.description: How many orbs are required to unlock the door to Ripto.

options.name: enable_open_world
options.description:
If on, Crush and Gulp do not require talismans.
Removes talisman items from the pool.
Effectively allows early access to each homeworld,
though swim and climb may logicially lock progression.

options.name: open_world_warp_unlocks
options.description:
The player will start with warps to all 3 worlds.
Only has an effect in open world mode.

options.name: start_with_abilities
options.description: The player will start with swim, climb, headbash.

options.name: wt_warp_options
options.description:
When warping to Winter Tundra from outside Crush or Gulp,
reroutes the warp to inside the castle. Logically grants access
to inner Winter Tundra.
Represents planned vanilla functionality that was cut.
Vanilla: Warps always go to outside the castle.
Door: Warps inside the castle if you have unlocked the
    door with headbash.
Wall Orb: Warps inside the castle if you have the WT wall orb.
Always: Warps always go to inside the castle. Logic will expect
    homeworld navigation via Winter Tundra's castle warp.

options.name: open_professor_door
options.description:
Automatically open the Professor's door in Autumn Plains,
which normally requires 8 orbs (normally bypassable with tricks).
This door also affects the behavior of the warp from Crush to
Autumn Plains. When the door is open, this warp will bring you
next to Gulp, allowing you to skip needing climb to access the second
half of Autumn Plains. This option is intended for use with
open world with open world warps, but can be used without.

options.name: level_lock_options
options.description:
Rules for unlocking each level.
Glimmer, homeworlds, and bosses always have their vanilla requirements.
If Moneybagssanity is off, sets prices to 0 to ensure beatable seeds.
Vanilla: Levels are available if you meet the vanilla requirements.
Keys: Levels are unlocked by finding "Unlock" items.
    Note: you may need to increase the number of locations (for example,
    by adding gem checks) to provide spots for the keys.

options.name: level_unlocks
options.description:
How many level unlocks the player starts with.
The player always has access to Glimmer, homeworlds, and bosses.
Only has an effect when level locks are on.

options.name: moneybags_settings
options.description:
Settings for Moneybags unlocks.
Vanilla - Pay Moneybags to progress as usual
Moneybagssanity - You cannot pay Moneybags at all and must        find unlock items to progress. Glimmer Bridge
    is excluded to avoid issues with early game randomization.

options.name: enable_filler_extra_lives
options.description: Adds extra lives to the item pool.

options.name: enable_destructive_spyro_filler
options.description:
Adds to the item pool an item temporarily powering up
Spyro so anything destructible he touches is destroyed.
Affects enemies, strong chests, breakable walls, etc.
Warning: Behavior is not consistent while charging.

options.name: enable_filler_color_change
options.description: Adds changing Spyro's color to the item pool.

options.name: enable_filler_big_head_mode
options.display_name: Enable Big Head and Flat Spyro Filler
options.description:
Adds turning on Big Head Mode and flat Spyro Mode
to the item pool.

options.name: enable_filler_heal_sparx
options.description:
Adds healing Sparx to the item pool.
Sparx must be alive to be healed.

options.name: trap_filler_percent
options.description: The percentage of filler items that will be traps.

options.name: enable_trap_damage_sparx
options.description:
Adds damaging Sparx to the item pool.
Cannot directly kill Spyro.

options.name: enable_trap_sparxless
options.description: Adds an item removing Sparx to the item pool.

options.name: enable_trap_invisibility
options.description:
Adds turning Spyro invisible briefly to the item pool.
Duckstation must be run in Interpreter mode for this to have any effect.

options.name: enable_trap_lazy_sparx
options.description: Adds making Sparx refuse to pick up gems for 30 seconds to the item pool.

options.name: double_jump_ability
options.description:
Settings for the unintended double jump ability, where jumping
then pressing square without letting go of jump gains extra height.
May impact logic on harder trick settings.
Duckstation must be run in Interpreter mode for this to have any effect.
Vanilla - Double Jump behaves normally.
In Pool - Adds Double Jump to the item pool.
    The ability is disabled until you acquire the item.
Off - Double Jump is disabled.

options.name: permanent_fireball_ability
options.description:
Settings for the Dragon Shores permanent fireball ability.
May impact logic, even on the easiest trick settings.
Vanilla - The fireball powerup in Dragon Shores behaves normally.
In Pool - Adds Permanent Fireball to the item pool.
    The Dragon Shores powerup does not work.
Off - Permanent Fireball is disabled.
    The Dragon Shores powerup does not work.
Start With - You begin the game with fireball, as in New Game Plus.

options.name: enable_progressive_sparx_health
options.description:
Start the game with lower max health
and add items to the pool to increase your max health.
Off - The game behaves normally.
Blue - Your max health starts at blue Sparx.
Green - Your max health starts at green Sparx.
Sparxless - Your max health starts at no Sparx.
True Sparxless - Your max health is permanently Sparxless.

options.name: enable_progressive_sparx_logic
options.description:
Certain Sparx health amounts are expected before
you are required to enter different levels.
Aquaria Towers, Crush, and Autumn Plains expects green Sparx.
Entering Skelos Badlands, Gulp, and Winter Tundra expects blue Sparx.
Entering Ripto expects gold Sparx.
This does nothing unless Enable Progressive Sparx Health Upgrades
is set to blue, green, or Sparxless,

options.name: sparx_gem_finder
options.description:
Settings for Sparx's gem finder ability.
Vanilla - Holding L1+R1+R2 has Sparx point to the nearest gem.
    Beating Ripto makes the effect permanent.
In Pool - Adds Sparx Gem Finder to the item pool.
    Behaves like Vanilla once you find the item.
Off - Removes the ability for Sparx to point to gems.

options.name: extended_sparx_range
options.description:
Settings for Extended Sparx range.
Vanilla - Beating Ripto doubles Sparx's range.
In Pool - Adds Extended Sparx Range to the item pool.
    This item doubles Sparx's range.
    Beating Ripto has no effect on your range.

options.name: extra_hit_point
options.display_name: Extra Hit Point in Item Pool
options.description:
Adds an extra hit point (equivalent to the in game cheat code)
to the item pool. This does not affect progressive health logic.
Has no effect when Progressive Sparx Health Upgrades are True Sparxless.

options.name: trick_difficulty
options.description:
Determines which tricks, if any, are in logic.
See https://github.com/Uroogla/S2AP/wiki/Tricks-and-Presets
Off: Only dev-intended strategies are expected.
Easy Tricks: Straightforward double jumps and fireball usage may be required.
Medium Tricks: Standard speedrunning tricks, including more difficult
    double jumps, may be required.
Custom: Choose exactly which tricks are in logic.

options.name: custom_tricks
options.description:
Set various tricks for logic.
Only used if Trick Difficulty is Custom.
Format as a comma-separated list of "nice" names:
["Sunny Beach Turtle Soup without Climb", "Crystal Glacier Bridge without Moneybags"].
A full list of supported tricks can be found at:
https://github.com/Uroogla/S2AP/blob/main/apworld/spyro2/LogicTricks.py
options: aquaria towers first tunnel with double jump
aquaria towers gems out of bounds
aquaria towers royal children out of bounds
aquaria towers talisman area with double jump
aquaria towers third button with fireball
autumn plains double jumps from zephyr
crystal glacier skip bridge with double jump
crystal glacier skip bridge with snowball proxy
glimmer indoor lamps with double jump
magma cone moneybags with double jump
magma cone pass start with double jump
magma cone pass start with nothing
magma cone popcorn area with double jump
magma cone second level with double jump
magma cone skip elevator with double jump
summer forest aquaria wall with double jump
summer forest aquaria wall with nothing
summer forest ledge orb with double jump
summer forest second half with double jump
summer forest second half with nothing
sunny beach middle ladders with double jump
sunny beach middle ladders with nothing
winter tundra glide into levels from out of bounds
winter tundra inside castle with double jump
winter tundra inside castle with penguin proxy
winter tundra out of bounds with double jump
winter tundra out of bounds with nothing
winter tundra swim from out of bounds
zephyr skip ladder with double jump

options.name: colossus_starting_goals
options.description: How many goals you start with in both Colossus orb challenges.

options.name: idol_easy_fish
options.description: Red fish behave the same as other types of fish in Idol Springs.

options.name: hurricos_easy_lightning_orbs
options.description: Lightning thieves do not steal the orbs in Hurricos.

options.name: breeze_required_gears
options.description: How many gears you must collect to complete the trolley orb.

options.name: scorch_bombo_settings
options.description:
Settings for the Bombo orb in Scorch.
Vanilla - Bombo behaves as normal.
First Only - Complete the first (shortest) path to complete the orb.
Attackless First Only - Complete the first (shortest) path to
    complete the orb. Bombo will not attack.

options.name: fracture_require_headbash
options.description:
Whether Hunter requires headbash to start Earthshaper Bash.
Without headbash, this orb can be completed with fireball
or the Fracture Easy Earthshapers setting.
This changes the orb's logic.

options.name: fracture_easy_earthshapers
options.description:
Removes the 7 earthshapers from the Alchemist area and
reduces the maximum number of spirit particles in the level.
Removes the headbash requirement from the Fracture Hills
all spirit particles check. The second orb still requires
headbash, unless Fracture Require Headbash is disabled.

options.name: magma_spyro_starting_popcorn
options.description: How many popcorn crystals you start with in each Hunter orb challenge.

options.name: magma_hunter_starting_popcorn
options.description: How many popcorn crystals Hunter starts with in each Hunter orb challenge.

options.name: shady_require_headbash
options.description:
Whether Free Hippos in Shady Oasis requires headbash to start.
Without headbash, this orb can be completed with fireball.
This changes the orb's logic.

options.name: easy_gulp
options.description: Spyro does double damage to Gulp.

options.name: portal_gem_collection_color
options.display_name: Portal and Gem Collection Text Color
options.description:
Changes the color of the number that appears when gems are collected,
as well as the text on portals.

options.name: gem_color
options.description:
Changes the color of gem types (and some other items in game).
Default: No changes.
Shuffle: Mixes up the colors of gem types.
Random Choice: Gem colors are randomly selected from a curated set of options.
True Random: Gem colors are completely random.  This probably won't look great.

options.name: musicsanity
options.description:
Shuffles most of the music in game.
Off: No changes.
Like With Like: Music is shuffled within its type:
    Homeworld, Normal Level, Boss.
Full: Music is shuffled regardless of type.

options.name: streamer_music
options.description:
Streaming platforms sometimes mute VODs with the Autumn Plains
or Winter Tundrea homeworld music.
Replaces these with Summer Forest.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.
options: Moneybags Unlock - Wall by Aquaria Towers

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.
options: Moneybags Unlock - Wall by Aquaria Towers

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"
options: Moneybags Unlock - Wall by Aquaria Towers

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
options: Moneybags Unlock - Wall by Aquaria Towers

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
